section: Chapter: The Magic Brush of Dreams

questions.question: Who gave Gopi the magic brush?
questions.correct_answer: A mysterious man sitting on a stone
questions.explanation: The man secretly gave Gopi a magic brush and told her to use it only to help poor people.

questions.question: What special power did the magic brush have?
questions.correct_answer: Anything Gopi painted with it became real
questions.explanation: The magic brush turned Gopi’s drawings into real objects, which helped the villagers.

questions.question: What was the first thing Gopi painted with the magic brush?
questions.explanation: When Gopi painted a bowl of khichdi, it became real and amazed the villagers.

questions.question: How did Gopi help the villagers with the magic brush?
questions.correct_answer: She painted useful items like a muffler, plough, shawl, and fan
questions.explanation: Gopi used the brush to create things that villagers needed in their daily lives.

questions.question: Who tried to force Gopi to paint treasures?
questions.explanation: The greedy Zamindar wanted Gopi to paint riches and a fortress for him.

questions.question: What did the Zamindar do when Gopi refused his order?
questions.explanation: The Zamindar became angry and ordered his men to capture Gopi and throw her into prison.

questions.question: How did Gopi escape from the prison?
questions.correct_answer: She painted a road and a horse
questions.explanation: Using the magic brush, Gopi created a road and a strong horse to escape from the prison.

questions.question: What obstacle did Gopi paint to stop the Zamindar’s men?
questions.explanation: The river prevented the Zamindar and his men from crossing and continuing the chase.

questions.question: How did the Zamindar react when Gopi threatened to paint a beast?
questions.correct_answer: He became frightened and ran away
questions.explanation: The Zamindar feared the beast and decided to stop chasing Gopi.

questions.question: What happened at the end of the poem?
questions.explanation: After escaping from the Zamindar, Gopi used the magic brush to create a feast and celebrate with the villagers.

notes.title: Gopi
notes.content: Gopi is a kind and imaginative girl who uses the magic brush only to help poor villagers.

notes.title: The Magic Brush
notes.content: The magic brush can turn drawings into real objects, symbolising creativity and responsibility.

notes.title: The Zamindar
notes.content: The Zamindar represents greed and misuse of power because he wants to use the brush for wealth and luxury.

notes.title: Gopi’s Escape
notes.content: Gopi uses her creativity and courage to escape from prison by painting a road, a horse, and a river.

notes.title: Ending of the Story
notes.content: The story ends with Gopi celebrating with villagers, showing that kindness and courage defeat greed.

facts.fact: The magic brush in the story symbolises the power of imagination and creativity.
Gopi uses the magic brush only to help poor people, following the promise she made.
The Zamindar’s greed causes the main conflict in the poem.
The story teaches that kindness and wisdom are stronger than greed.
